MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

shifts.trend: Latest filing introduces tightness language absent from prior
shifts.note: auto-derived (new tightness — single-quarter, lower confidence)

shifts.trend: Supply-tightness language present in both latest filings
shifts.note: auto-derived from filing keyword presence

shifts.trend: Latest filing introduces tightness language absent from prior
shifts.note: auto-derived (new tightness — single-quarter, lower confidence)

AMKR.headline: Revenue inflected sharply in Q1; capex tripling YoY for Arizona advanced-packaging fab is the dominant story.

shifts.trend: Revenue +6.2% FY25 → +27.5% Q1 2026; all four end-markets positive in Q1 (comms +42%, auto +28%, computing +19%, consumer +4%)
shifts.note: FY25 was drag from Vietnam ramp; Q1 shows underlying demand recovery
Arizona Facility construction is the primary driver; CHIPS Act ITC raised to 35%

shifts.trend: Gross margin 14.0% FY25 → 14.2% Q1 2026 (+2.3pts YoY); Vietnam ramp inefficiencies partially resolving
shifts.note: Still structurally low-margin business; upside from volume leverage as Arizona ramps

shifts.trend: Apple 29.8%, Qualcomm 11.1%, top-10 = 72% across both filings — no change
shifts.note: Extreme Apple dependency unchanged; Arizona Facility partly Apple-specific

AMKR.verdict: Story intact. Arizona fab capex step-change ($0.9B → $2.5-3.0B) is a long-duration call on advanced packaging demand; near-term margin compression from construction is the key risk to watch.

AMAT.headline: HBM driving mix shift from logic to DRAM; China revenue declining and a $253M BIS settlement clouded Q1 margins.

shifts.trend: Foundry/logic 67% → 62% of SemiSys; DRAM 26% → 34% — memory gaining share as HBM tech-transition spend accelerates
shifts.note: NAND weak both periods; memory momentum is real

shifts.trend: China $8.5B (-16%) FY25 → $2.1B (-7%) Q1 FY26; Taiwan surged from 16% to 25% of mix
shifts.note: China headwind softening but still negative; Taiwan compensating

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $28.4B (+4%) → Q1 FY26 $7.0B (-2% YoY) — sequential decel from prior year strength in foundry
shifts.note: $253M BIS settlement charge in Q1 masked underlying demand picture

shifts.trend: Operating margin 30.4% FY25 → 26.1% Q1 FY26 (-4.3pts); gross margin 48.7% → 49.0% (+0.2pts, stable)
shifts.note: Margin hit is one-time BIS settlement; underlying GM held; opex deleverage the real pressure

AMAT.verdict: Thesis intact but near-term clouded by the BIS settlement charge; DRAM/HBM mix shift is the right signal to track — that's accelerating while logic weakens.

LRCX.headline: Memory mix accelerating as DRAM/HBM investment surges; revenue +9% QoQ with foundry share falling and Korea leading.

shifts.trend: Memory 34% → 39% of mix; DRAM strengthened and NVM investment added; Foundry fell 59% → 54%
shifts.note: HBM capex cycle driving memory intensity higher at expense of mature foundry

shifts.trend: Revenue $5.34B (flat QoQ in Dec) → $5.84B (+9% QoQ in Mar); 9M revenue $16.51B vs $13.26B (+24% YoY)
shifts.note: Sequential momentum re-established after flat Dec quarter

shifts.trend: China 35% Dec qtr → 34% Mar qtr — roughly flat; Korea jumped 20% → 23% as memory spend concentrates in SK Hynix/Samsung
shifts.note: China still elevated but Korea stepping up as offsetting memory geography

shifts.trend: Gross margin 49.6% → 49.8% (+0.2pts) on better factory efficiencies; customer-support revenue $1.99B → $2.11B (+6%) growing the installed base annuity

LRCX.verdict: Story intact. DRAM/HBM is the right driver and it's accelerating. Foundry softness is the offset but memory intensity is what matters for the bottleneck thesis.

KLAC.headline: Revenue hit record $3.42B in Q3 on HBM/DRAM intensity; China concentration dropped from 30% to 24% of mix as Korea surged +80%.

shifts.trend: Revenue $3.30B (+7% YoY) Q2 → $3.42B (+11% YoY) Q3 — record; services 24% → 23% of mix as products grew faster
shifts.note: DRAM/HBM + advanced packaging called out as primary drivers both quarters

shifts.trend: China $995M (30.2%, -9% YoY) Q2 → $830M (24%, +5% YoY) Q3; 9M China $3.09B flat — export controls still constraining
shifts.note: China share fell 6pts in one quarter; Korea offsetting at +80%

shifts.trend: Gross margin 61.4% (+1.1pts) Q2 → 61.1% (-0.5pts QoQ) Q3; DRAM chip cost pressure on image computers flagged as transitory
shifts.note: Mgmt confident in 'transitory' framing; core measurement margin structure intact

shifts.trend: Repurchases $548M Q2 → $626M Q3; dividend raised 17th consecutive year to $2.30/share/qtr; $7B added to buyback program ($10.31B remaining)
shifts.note: Signal of mgmt confidence in earnings durability

KLAC.verdict: Story intact and strengthening. Record revenue, accelerating HBM intensity, China exposure shrinking — exactly the right direction. Margin headwind (image-computer DRAM) is flagged as transitory and appears small relative to volume growth.

AMD.headline: Data Center re-accelerated to +57% YoY in Q1 from +32% in FY25; gross margin expanded 3pts to 53%; MI325 China licenses granted with 25% re-import tariff attached.

shifts.trend: DC segment +32% FY25 → +57% Q1 FY26; total revenue +34% FY25 → +38% Q1; DC now 56% of Q1 revenue vs 48% FY25
shifts.note: MI350 GPU demand + EPYC server momentum driving acceleration; OpenAI 6 GW deal signed Oct 2025

shifts.trend: Gross margin 50% FY25 → 53% Q1 FY26 (+3pts YoY); DC operating income margin 22% → 28%
shifts.note: Better mix from higher DC segment and clearing of MI308 export charge headwind

shifts.trend: MI308 inventory charge $800M reversed partially ($360M) in FY25; Q1 FY26 MI325 licenses granted — still subject to 25% US re-import tariff
shifts.note: China revenue $7.8B (22%) in FY25; partial export access restored but tariff creates ongoing margin friction

shifts.trend: R&D +39% to $2.4B and MG&A +41% to $1.3B Q1 FY26 — aggressive AI headcount build-out signals longer-term confidence
shifts.note: Not capex in the traditional sense but OpEx investment that constrains near-term margins

AMD.verdict: Re-acceleration is real — DC growing 57% YoY Q1 with improving margins. OpenAI 6 GW warrant/purchase deal provides multi-year backlog visibility. China tariff is a drag but not a thesis-killer at current volume levels.

AVGO.headline: Top-5 customer concentration jumped from ~40% to ~50% in one quarter; semiconductor now 65% of revenue as AI XPU demand displaces software mix.

shifts.trend: Top-5 end customers ~40% FY25 → ~50% Q1 FY26; one distributor 32% FY25 → 42% Q1 — implies single hyperscaler XPU order dominance
shifts.note: This is the primary new risk: extreme concentration in a handful of XPU customers

shifts.trend: Semi solutions 55% → 65% of revenue; semi +22% FY25 → +52% Q1 as AI accelerators ramp
shifts.note: VMware software synergies normalizing; AI XPU becoming the growth engine

shifts.trend: Revenue +24% FY25 → +29% Q1 FY26; semiconductor solutions $36.9B FY25 → $12.5B Q1 run-rate ($50B annualized)
Gross margin 68% FY25 → 68% Q1 FY26 — flat despite higher semi mix as VM software margin dilutes the semi benefit
shifts.note: Infrastructure software $27B FY25 → $6.8B Q1 (+1%) is plateauing after VMware integration

AVGO.verdict: Growth story remains intact but concentration risk has risen materially — one distributor at 42% of revenue is a single-quarter cliff risk. Worth watching whether this normalizes or persists in subsequent quarters.

MRVL.headline: Data center +46% for the full year, 74% of revenue; acquisitions of Celestial AI and XConn post-year-end extend the photonic interconnect and CXL switching roadmap.

shifts.note: Custom AI ASIC and electro-optics the twin drivers; semiconductor silicon photonics cited explicitly in roadmap

shifts.trend: Gross margin 51.6% Q3 → 51.0% FY26 — structurally recovered from 41% prior year; absorption and mix stable
shifts.note: Prior year had $358M restructuring impairments; now at clean-run margin level

shifts.trend: Distributor A 38% Q3 → 37% FY26; Direct A 11% Q3 → 14% FY26 — direct customer share inching up
shifts.note: Top-10 historically ~81% — consistent hyperscaler concentration

shifts.trend: Celestial AI (photonic fabric scale-up) + XConn (PCIe/CXL switch) both closed Feb 2026 to fill roadmap gaps; explicitly building custom HBM integration capability
shifts.note: M&A is filling interconnect gaps that organic development would take years to solve

MRVL.verdict: Story intact and optionality expanding. DC +46% with photonic M&A positions MRVL uniquely at the custom-silicon + scale-up interconnect intersection — the question is execution on integrating three acquisitions simultaneously.

GOOGL.headline: Cloud revenue accelerated to +63% in Q1 (vs +36% FY25); RPO nearly doubled to $467.6B in a single quarter as multi-gigawatt TPU supply deals were booked.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud +36% YoY FY25 → +63% YoY Q1 2026; Cloud operating income $13.9B FY25 → $6.6B Q1 (33% margin, 3x YoY)
shifts.note: Fastest-accelerating hyperscaler cloud revenue across the tracked universe

shifts.trend: Capex $91.4B FY25 → $35.7B Q1 2026 alone ($143B annualized); 2026 guide 'significantly increases' from already-record $91.4B
shifts.note: Multi-GW TPU supply agreements include credit backstops for third-party DCs/power

shifts.trend: RPO $242.8B at YE25 → $467.6B at Q1 end (+93% in one quarter); $462.3B is Google Cloud — 'multi-gigawatt' TPU hardware supply agreements signed
shifts.note: Supply agreements revenue starts late 2026 majority in 2027 — demand already committed

shifts.trend: $40B committed to a private company (OpenAI?) Mar 2026; $16B funded into Waymo — Alphabet diversifying large capital commitments beyond GCP

GOOGL.verdict: Most explosive quarter across hyperscaler filings. Cloud +63% with 33% margins and RPO nearly doubled — the market signal is extraordinary demand for compute. Capex trajectory ($91B → implied $140B+) is the key investor question.

COHR.headline: D&C segment accelerated each quarter (+34% → +41%); 'scale across' interconnect named as new demand category in Q3; pure-play optics portfolio forming via portfolio divestitures.

shifts.trend: D&C revenue $1.21B (+34%) Q2 → $1.36B (+41%) Q3; segment profit +44% → +49% YoY
shifts.note: AI datacenter + DCI driving both periods; 'scale across' added as new category in Q3

shifts.trend: Gross margin 37% (+140bps) Q2 → 38% (+243bps) Q3; yield improvements and pricing optimization each quarter
shifts.note: Industrial divestitures improving consolidated mix toward higher-margin D&C

shifts.trend: Aerospace divested Sep 2025; Munich divested Jan 2026; D&C now 75% of revenue (vs 73% in Q2) — portfolio narrowing toward AI optics
shifts.note: Industrial -10% Q2 → -16% Q3 as divestitures reduce the segment

shifts.trend: Management added 'scale across' inter-datacenter AI interconnect as a demand driver in Q3 — first explicit naming, expanding the total addressable market narrative

COHR.verdict: Thesis intact. Every metric moving in the right direction: revenue accelerating, margins expanding, portfolio simplifying toward AI optics, and the addressable market growing (scale-across). Industrial headwind will fully resolve as divestitures close.

FN.headline: DCI the fastest-growing line at +90% in Q3; revenue growth accelerated to +39%; HPC emerging as a disclosed segment.

shifts.note: DCI $142M → $197M (+90% YoY) is the star — inter-DC AI scale-across fabric ramp

shifts.trend: DCI $142.2M → $196.9M (+90% YoY); Telecom $412.2M → $431.4M (+42.5%); HPC disclosed at $85.6M → $106.7M; Datacom flat both quarters
shifts.note: Datacom flat/declining as speed mix shifts to DCI; HPC emerging as AI-relevant line

shifts.trend: 4 customers each >10% in both Q2 and Q3 (combined 59.1% Q2); Nokia/Infinera + Lumentum/NeoPhotonics consolidation flagged as ongoing risk
shifts.note: More concentrated than 2 customers a year ago — tracking risk direction

shifts.trend: Gross margin 12.2% Q2 → 11.9% Q3 (-30bps) on inventory build for higher demand + Chonburi capex ramp; operating income +43.7% → +52.1% YoY as volume absorbs fixed costs
shifts.note: Thin gross margins structurally — the leverage is operating margin

FN.verdict: Story intact. DCI at +90% is the clearest signal of hyperscaler AI scale-across demand. Customer consolidation risk is real but manageable; thin margins mean execution on volume is everything.

MXL.headline: Recovery solidifying: revenue +30% FY25 and +43% Q1 YoY; optical DSP for 800G→1.6T transition is the explicit next-leg thesis with infrastructure now the lead segment.

shifts.trend: Revenue $467.6M (+30%) FY25 → $137.2M Q1 2026 (+43% YoY); Infrastructure (optical) grew $34.3M in FY25 and $36.3M in Q1 — consistent strongest segment
shifts.note: Recovery from 2023-24 cyclical trough; infrastructure optical DSPs leading each period

shifts.trend: Largest customer 13% Q1 (vs prior years where top was >20%); top-10 = 56% Q1 vs 65% FY25 — diversification improving
shifts.note: Lower concentration de-risks the thesis somewhat vs hyperscaler peers

shifts.trend: Broadband 44% of FY25 revenue; Infrastructure growing as optical DSP wins; Connectivity recovering; Industrial/multi-market normalizing
shifts.note: Broadband/connectivity are cyclical; infrastructure is the structural AI-linked growth driver

shifts.trend: 10-K explicitly documents 400G → 800G → 1.6T (1600Gbps) transition as the optical DSP roadmap; hyperscaler switch interconnect transition is the demand catalyst
shifts.note: 1.6T cycle not yet fully in revenue — this is an upcoming inflection to monitor

MXL.verdict: Recovery intact with the 1.6T cycle providing the next earnings catalyst. Optical DSP traction is real but not yet the dominant revenue driver; monitor infrastructure segment growth as a leading indicator.

ANET.headline: Revenue accelerated to +35% in Q1; deferred revenue +$826M in a single quarter as AI Ethernet trials convert; RPO hit $7.7B from $6.1B at year-end.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $9.0B (+28.6%) → Q1 FY26 $2.71B (+35.1%) — acceleration; Americas jumped to 84.5% from 79.7% mix
shifts.note: AI and Cloud Titan customers 48% of FY25 revenue; US concentration rising with AI titan build

shifts.trend: Deferred revenue $5.37B → $6.20B (+$826M) in one quarter; RPO $6.1B → $7.7B; binding product orders $968M — trials converting to committed capacity
shifts.note: Acceptance-period contracts mean shipments precede revenue recognition — deferred revenue is a demand proxy

shifts.trend: Gross margin 64.1% FY25 → 61.9% Q1 (-2.2pts) from large-customer mix and memory/silicon supply tightening
shifts.note: Memory and silicon supply cited explicitly as gross-margin pressure — cost not fully passed through

shifts.trend: Two end customers 26% and 16% of FY25 revenue; Americas 79.7% → 84.5% of Q1 revenue — US titan concentration tightening
shifts.note: High-quality concentration (hyperscalers) but still a single-point risk

ANET.verdict: Demand signal is exceptional — $826M deferred revenue in one quarter and RPO +26% vs prior year-end. Margin compression from memory supply is the watch item; if it persists beyond Q1, it chips at the valuation premium.

CSCO.headline: Networking product growth accelerated from +15% to +21% across the two quarters as AI Infrastructure ordering continues; Silicon One purchase commitments rising each period.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +8% Q1 → +10% Q2; Networking +15% (+$1.0B) Q1 → +21% (+$1.4B) Q2 on AI Infrastructure, Service Provider Routing, DC Switching
shifts.note: Security offsetting with declines (-4% Q2) from Splunk cloud shift; excluding security, product growth stronger

shifts.trend: Inventory +7% and purchase commitments +10% Q1 vs FYE; Silicon One commitments rising Q2 — proactive supply chain build for hyperscaler orders
shifts.note: Mgmt flagging obsolescence risk on commitments — double-edged; signals genuine demand pull

shifts.trend: Product GM -0.6pts Q1 on mix/pricing → +0.2pts Q2 recovery on productivity; diluted EPS +31% Q2 on tax rate improvement (12.9% vs 15.9%)
shifts.note: Security mix dragging product margins; AI Networking mix improving

shifts.trend: RPO $42.87B Q1 → implied $43-44B Q2; software revenue $5.7B flat both periods; subscription revenue flat — AI networking is product not subscription
shifts.note: The AI infrastructure revenue is flowing through product not recurring software — future transition risk

CSCO.verdict: Acceleration is real (+21% Networking in Q2). Silicon One is the key signal — if purchase commitments keep building, the hyperscaler AI Infrastructure order wave is durable. Security drag is a known headwind that doesn't affect the bottleneck thesis.

CLS.headline: HPS networking switch revenue grew +81% FY25 and +63% Q1; Enterprise end-market inflected from -19% to +101% as next-gen AI compute ramp resumed.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +42% FY25 CCS → +76% Q1 FY26 CCS; HPS $5.0B (41%) FY25 → ~$1.7B (42%) Q1 — proportionally rising each period
shifts.note: HPS networking switch the dominant driver both periods; acceleration continuing

shifts.trend: Enterprise end-market -19% FY25 (hyperscaler AI compute tech transition) → +101% Q1 FY26 (next-gen ramp restarted); Communications +81% → +69%
shifts.note: Enterprise flip from -19% to +101% is the key new development — AI compute programs re-ramping

shifts.trend: CCS segment margin 8.4% Q4 FY25 → 8.6% Q1 FY26; Q1 adjusted EPS $2.16 beat high end of $1.95-2.15 guide
shifts.note: Operating leverage on volume growth driving margin expansion

shifts.trend: Revolver upsized $750M → $1.75B; Term A Loan refinanced to $250M with April 2031 maturity — balance sheet built for sustained scale-up
shifts.note: Debt facility expansion signals management confidence in multi-year demand visibility

CLS.verdict: Strongest EMS story in the stack. Enterprise AI compute ramp resuming (+101%) alongside HPS networking (+63%) means both hyperscaler demand vectors are now pulling simultaneously. Leverage expansion is prudent given demand backlog.

GEV.headline: RPO surged $13B in a single quarter to $163.3B; Power EBITDA margin expanded from 14.7% to 16.3% on favorable pricing; heavy-duty turbine order pace at historically elevated levels.

shifts.trend: RPO $150.2B YE25 → $163.3B Q1 (sequential +$13B, +9%); equipment RPO $64.2B → $75.9B; HA-Turbine annual orders: 41 (2023) → 68 (2024) → 110 (2025)
shifts.note: Order cadence at 2-3x prior cycle peak; 2028+ deliveries already being booked

shifts.trend: Power segment EBITDA margin 14.7% FY25 → 16.3% Q1 2026 (+1.6pts); Q1 free cash flow $4.8B vs $1.0B prior year
shifts.note: Favorable pricing (backlog-priced at higher rates) and higher volume driving margin expansion

shifts.trend: Revenue $38.1B FY25 (+9%) → $9.3B Q1 2026 (+16%); Gas Power Q1 +13%; adjusted EBITDA Q1 $896M (+$439M YoY)
Prolec GE (50% stake, $5.3B) closed Feb 2026; $5B cumulative R&D 2025-2028 committed; Electrification segment benefiting from transformer/HVDC demand
shifts.note: M&A filling transformer gap that is equally supply-constrained alongside turbines

GEV.verdict: Cleanest bottleneck story in the power layer. RPO 3x pre-AI cycle levels with backlog extending to 2028+; margin expansion through better pricing confirms scarcity. Prolec GE adds transformer manufacturing — the other constrained power component.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog grew to $14.5B (+44% YoY) with organic orders +42% in Q1; Boyd Thermal acquisition ($9.5B) pending to add liquid cooling.

shifts.trend: EA backlog $13.2B (+31% YoY) FY25 → $14.5B (+44%) Q1 2026; book-to-bill 1.2x both periods; organic orders +42% Q1
shifts.note: Backlog acceleration confirms data-center-driven demand is not slowing

shifts.trend: EA organic growth rate accelerating: +16% FY25 → +20% Q1; EG +9% FY25 → +21% Q1 — both electrical segments accelerating
shifts.note: Data center is primary driver for EA; EG cited machine OEM/residential growth added to DC

shifts.trend: EA operating margin 29.9% FY25 (-20bps) → 25.6% Q1 (-480bps YoY); commodity/tariff inflation absorbing volume tailwind
shifts.note: Timing factor — Q1 typically seasonally weaker; 29.9% is more representative annual-level profitability

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal ($9.5B, liquid cooling) signed Nov 2025, expected to close Q2 2026; prior: Fibrebond (modular DC power enclosures) + Resilient (solid-state transformers)
shifts.note: Eaton deliberately building a data-center-adjacent M&A portfolio across cooling, power, enclosures

ETN.verdict: Demand is clearly accelerating (orders +42% organic in Q1); margin compression is a cost-inflation concern worth tracking but not thesis-breaking. Boyd Thermal close will significantly expand liquid cooling exposure — a new growth vector.

VRT.headline: Americas revenue accelerated to +53% in Q1 with margin expansion to 27.0%; gross margin jumped 400bps to 37.7%; backlog doubled YoY and capex guidance more than doubled for FY26.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +27.7% FY25 → +30.1% Q1 FY26; Americas +41.9% FY25 → +53.1% Q1; Americas is 68% of Q1 revenue
shifts.note: US data-center concentration is the dominant driver; APAC and EMEA much weaker

shifts.trend: Gross margin 36.3% (-30bps) FY25 → 37.7% (+400bps) Q1 FY26; Americas operating margin 26.8% → 27.0%; operating profit +51%
shifts.note: Volume leverage offsetting tariffs; Americas margin expansion is real

shifts.trend: Combined order backlog $15.0B at YE25 (doubled from $7.2B); Q1 FY26 end backlog not disclosed but 'very robust AI/HPC demand' noted by mgmt
shifts.note: Backlog >1 year of revenue signals multi-year visibility

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $226.4M → FY26 guided $425-525M (2x YoY); Q1 capex $114M vs annualized $425-525M — weighted to H2
shifts.note: Doubling capex signals management confidence in sustained demand

VRT.verdict: Strongest operational execution in the cooling/power stack. Americas +53% with margin expansion and doubling capex is the right combination. Thesis intact; the key risk is EMEA/APAC weakness which could weigh on mix if the US AI buildout softens.

GNRC.headline: C&I segment re-accelerated to +28% in Q1 on data center demand; segment restructured to Residential/C&I to align with the two-speed business; Enercon acquired for custom hyperscale power.

shifts.trend: Total sales -2% FY25 → +12% Q1 FY26; C&I +4.9% FY25 → +28% Q1 on data center; Residential -6.8% FY25 → +1% Q1 — residential stabilizing
shifts.note: C&I acceleration driven by data center specifically, not general industrial

shifts.trend: Adj EBITDA margin 17.0% FY25 → ~18.2% Q1 ($193M on $1.06B sales); C&I margin 11.4% FY25 → 13.0% Q1
shifts.note: Data center mix improving C&I margins; Residential operational efficiencies driving 25.1% margin

shifts.trend: FY25 10-K: large-megawatt backup generator market 'significantly supply-constrained'; Enercon acquired Q1 2026 for custom power equipment for hyperscale data centers
shifts.note: Supply constraint language from 10-K consistent with C&I demand pull seen in Q1

shifts.trend: Segment reorganization: Domestic/International → Residential/C&I in March 2026; signals management treating data center as the primary C&I growth driver worthy of separate strategic tracking

GNRC.verdict: Inflection is real — C&I at +28% with improving margins. Large-megawatt data center generators are a new market that GNRC explicitly says is supply-constrained; Enercon adds custom power to the product mix. Residential remains a drag but is stabilizing.

HUBB.headline: Both segments accelerated in Q1 (ES organic +10.6%, US organic +6.8%); total backlog $2.16B covers 2026 deliveries with data center as the primary Electrical Solutions driver.

shifts.trend: ES organic +7.2% FY25 → +10.6% Q1 FY26; US organic +1.1% FY25 → +6.8% Q1; total revenue +3.8% FY25 → +11% Q1
shifts.note: Acceleration broad-based across both segments; FY25 was partly burdened by AMI grid automation weakness

shifts.trend: Total firm backlog $2.16B (+14% vs YE24); substantially all targeted for delivery in 2026 — strong near-term visibility
shifts.note: Backlog conversion risk is low given firm nature of orders

shifts.trend: Adj operating margin 22.5% (+80bps) FY25 → +110bps Q1 FY26; ES adj margin 20.2% FY25 → 16.4% Q1 (-30bps on commodity/tariff)
shifts.note: ES margin dip is Q1 seasonal + tariff; full-year trajectory should improve as mix and volume offset

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions data center vertical explicitly named as primary growth driver in both filings; Utility T&D as secondary; Grid Automation (AMI) still lagging

HUBB.verdict: Solid execution with acceleration. At current growth rates Hubb is a steady compounder in the data-center electrical infrastructure space. The key risk is commodity/tariff cost pressure on ES margins; the thesis doesn't break at current levels.

PWR.headline: Revenue +26% Q1 YoY as backlog hit $48.47B (+10% sequentially) and RPO reached $26.24B — order pace is accelerating not decelerating.

shifts.note: Sequential backlog and RPO both growing — demand is not being fulfilled faster than it arrives

shifts.trend: Revenue $28.5B FY25 (+20.3%) → $7.87B Q1 2026 (+26.3% YoY); operating income +41.7% Q1; gross margin 14.1% (vs 13.4%)
shifts.note: All metrics accelerating — pricing leverage improving as backlog pricing catches up

shifts.trend: Operating margin 10.3% Electric FY25 (flat) → Electric-driven Q1 gross margin 14.1% (+70bps); net income Q1 +55% YoY
shifts.note: Underground segment margin expanded from 5.7% to 7.3% FY25 as DSI acquisition scaled

shifts.trend: $3.30B deployed in acquisitions FY25 (DSI, others); DSI + CEI provide turnkey DC infrastructure capability (inside DC, HV substation, generation)
shifts.note: M&A completing end-to-end DC capability; incremental organic growth from these new capabilities

PWR.verdict: Best combination of backlog + growth acceleration in the infrastructure stack. $48.47B backlog at rising margins with data center as the explicit primary catalyst. Thesis intact and strengthening.

NVT.headline: Systems Protection organic growth surged from ~17% FY25 to +50.1% Q1 as data-center infrastructure demand pulled sharply; segment margin expanded 220bps to 22.7%.

shifts.trend: SP organic growth ~17% FY25 → +50.1% Q1 2026; total revenue +29.5% FY25 → +53.5% Q1 (organic +12.6% → organic +34.4%)
shifts.note: ~46pts of Q1 SP organic growth from infrastructure/data centers — not a rounding error

shifts.trend: SP segment margin 20.7% FY25 → 22.7% Q1 (+2pts) on volume leverage; consolidated gross margin 37.7% (-2.5pts) FY25 → 35.9% (-2.9pts) Q1 on tariffs
shifts.note: SP segment-level improvement is real; consolidated GM headwind is tariff/material cost at group level

shifts.trend: Avail EPG acquisition $1.0B May 2025; Thermal Management divested to Brookfield $1.6B Jan 2025; net portfolio rotation toward data-center enclosures/liquid cooling
shifts.note: Disposed thermal to acquire higher-growth DC enclosures — structural bet on the power-dense cooling transition

shifts.trend: No material customer-concentration disclosure change across the two filings; infrastructure vertical remains primary growth vertical in both

NVT.verdict: Inflection confirmed — Q1 SP organic +50% is exceptional. EPG acquisition adding $121M in Q1 SP suggests acquisition is integrating well. Tariff headwind on consolidated GM is the watch item but doesn't threaten the demand thesis.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center sales doubled their YoY step-up ($67M → $130M); margin pressure from capacity ramp is easing; announced pure-play data center cooling pivot via Performance Tech spin.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions segment +24% Q2 → +51% Q3; data center YoY increment +$67.4M Q2 → +$130.0M Q3 — doubled; 9M DC product sales up $221.7M YoY
shifts.note: NA and European hyperscale/colocation both explicitly cited

shifts.trend: CS segment operating margin 13.7% Q2 (-440bps) → 15.3% Q3 (-200bps) — ramp inefficiencies beginning to normalize
shifts.note: Trajectory improving each quarter; mgmt expects further normalization as capacity matures

shifts.trend: Jan 2026 RMT announced: Performance Technologies spun into Gentherm, retaining Climate Solutions as pure-play data center + commercial HVAC/refrigeration company
shifts.note: Strategic transformation — post-close MOD becomes the cleanest pure-play DC cooling story in the stack

shifts.trend: Incremental $100M capex announced Q2 → incremental data center manufacturing lease signed Q3; $182M in acquisitions (AbsolutAire, LB White, Climate by Design) adding capacity

MOD.verdict: Significant structural transformation underway. RMT completion will create a pure-play DC cooling company with accelerating revenue; until close there is execution risk. Data center demand acceleration is real and confirmed; margin normalization is the near-term positive surprise.

MSFT.headline: Azure held at 39-40% growth for two straight quarters; capex accelerated from $29.9B to $30.9B per quarter; commercial RPO stable at $625-627B.

shifts.trend: Azure +39% Q2 → +40% Q3; Microsoft Cloud $51.5B (+26%) Q2 → $54.5B (+29%) Q3 — minor acceleration in cloud aggregate
shifts.note: Azure growth stable at the elevated 39-40% range; OpenAI partnership remains strategic anchor

shifts.trend: Capex $29.9B Q2 → $30.9B Q3; 9M total $80.1B vs $40.9B prior year (+96%); trajectory continuing to increase
shifts.note: Doubled YoY on a 9-month basis; no sign of moderation

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO $625B Q2 → $627B Q3 (+99% YoY both); OpenAI recapitalization generated $5.9B non-operating gain (Microsoft diluted into profit on conversion)
shifts.note: RPO stable at record levels — committed demand not decelerating despite high base

shifts.trend: Cloud gross margin under pressure both quarters from AI infrastructure investments; operating income +21% Q2, +20% Q3 — stable pace

MSFT.verdict: Steady execution at the largest scale. Azure stable at 39-40% is impressive given the base; capex acceleration is the watch item — $80B for 9 months vs $41B prior year. Thesis intact; cloud gross margin pressure from AI infra is the key margin risk.

META.headline: FY26 capex guide raised from $115-135B to $125-145B in Q1; ad pricing accelerated (+12% vs +9%); non-cancelable infrastructure commitments $237.7B as Meta locks in compute for a decade.

shifts.trend: Capex $72.2B FY25 → $19.8B Q1 2026 ($79B annualized); FY26 full-year guide raised to $125-145B (from $115-135B at year-end) — 2nd consecutive increase
shifts.note: Meta is the most aggressive capex inflator in the hyperscaler group; off-balance-sheet leases $182.9B not yet commenced

shifts.trend: Revenue +22% FY25 → +33% Q1 2026; ad impressions +12% → +19% YoY; price per ad +9% → +12%
shifts.note: Both volume and price components accelerating — healthy demand signal for ad spend

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable commitments $237.7B at Q1 end; $42.3B due 2026, $47.7B in 2027; added $24B in multi-year infra contracts in April 2026 post-quarter
shifts.note: Commitment scale is unprecedented; implies decade-long infrastructure buildout already contracted

shifts.trend: Reality Labs op loss $19.2B FY25 → $4.03B Q1 2026 (tracking similar for FY26); tax rate normalized to 13-16% vs 30% FY25 anomaly
shifts.note: RL remains a significant cash drain; OBBBA tax anomaly in FY25 is not repeating

META.verdict: Ad business accelerating while capex is the most aggressive in the peer group. $237.7B in non-cancelable commitments is the best leading indicator for sustained infrastructure demand across the AI stack. Thesis strongly intact.

AMZN.headline: AWS RPO leapt from $244B to $364B in a single quarter after OpenAI $100B expansion; Q1 capex $43.2B (+78% YoY); AWS margin expanded to 37.7% even as spending accelerates.

shifts.trend: AWS RPO $244B at YE25 → $364B at Q1 2026 end (+49% in one quarter); OpenAI expanded commitment by $100B; Anthropic $5B preferred + $20B facility post-quarter
shifts.note: Committed demand expanding far faster than capacity can be deployed — backlog is growing, not clearing

shifts.trend: Cash capex $128.3B FY25 (vs $77.7B FY24) → $43.2B Q1 2026 alone (+78% YoY Q1); management expects 2026 to increase further
shifts.note: On pace for ~$170B+ annualized if Q1 run-rate sustained

shifts.trend: AWS +20% YoY FY25 → +28% YoY Q1 2026; AWS operating income $45.6B FY25 → $14.16B Q1 (37.7% margin vs 35.4% FY25)
shifts.note: Revenue growth accelerating AND margins expanding — ideal combination

shifts.trend: AWS operating margin 35.4% FY25 → 37.7% Q1 2026 (+2.3pts) despite record capex; structural improvement from price/mix
shifts.note: Margin expansion while doubling capex is rare — signals pricing power and operational leverage

AMZN.verdict: Best AWS print in recent memory: growth accelerating, margins expanding, RPO surging. $100B OpenAI expansion is the single largest commercial cloud commitment disclosed. Thesis strongly intact; AWS is the clearest bottleneck in the hyperscaler layer.

ORCL.headline: OCI revenue growth accelerated from +68% to +84%; total revenue growth +14% → +22%; 9-month capex $39.2B is 3x prior year; $47.7B in senior notes issued to fund buildout.

shifts.trend: OCI (cloud infrastructure) +68% Q2 → +84% Q3; total revenue +14% Q2 → +22% Q3 — acceleration across both quarters
shifts.note: Oracle is catching up to AWS/Azure in cloud velocity; AI workload demand driving OCI utilization

shifts.trend: H1 capex $20.5B → 9M capex $39.2B (3.2x prior year); trend to continue 'next few fiscal years' per mgmt; Q3 alone ~$18.7B
shifts.note: Sustained high capex run-rate; Oracle financing via debt ($42.7B senior notes YTD + $5B convert)

shifts.trend: RPO $523.3B Q2 → $552.6B Q3 — massive customer commitments that dwarf annual revenue ($70B run-rate); weighted-avg contract life multi-year
shifts.note: RPO 7-8x annual revenue suggests demand committed years ahead; execution and capacity are the constraints

shifts.trend: Issued $42.7B senior notes + $5B mandatory convert + ATM offering in FY26; capex debt-funded while buybacks minimal ($95M)
shifts.note: Leverage rising substantially; manageable while OCI revenue accelerating but a risk if growth disappoints

ORCL.verdict: OCI acceleration (+84%) with RPO 7x revenue is an extraordinary demand signal. Leverage is rising but tolerable given revenue trajectory. Key risk: can Oracle actually build data center capacity fast enough to monetize the $552B RPO?

CRWV.headline: RPO more than doubled in a single quarter ($60.7B → $98.8B); Microsoft concentration fell from 67% to 45% as Meta emerged at 20%; raised $7.8B post-quarter at extreme leverage.

shifts.trend: RPO $60.7B FY25 → $98.8B Q1 2026 (+63% in one quarter); 850 MW active power FY25 → 3.1 GW contracted; demand well ahead of deployable capacity
shifts.note: CoreWeave is supply-constrained not demand-constrained — power and GPU delivery are the binding limits

shifts.trend: Microsoft 67% FY25 → 45% Q1 2026; Meta 20% Q1 2026 (not in top-2 in FY25); OpenAI $11.9B through Oct 2030 + $6.5B 2031 — diversification accelerating
shifts.note: Positive direction: 67% single-customer concentration was the primary risk; 45% and declining is much better

shifts.trend: Revenue $5.1B FY25 (+168%) → $2.08B Q1 2026 (+112% YoY); note: Q1 run-rate implies $8.3B annualized — strong sequential step-up
shifts.note: Growth remains triple-digit despite the high base; take-or-pay contract structure provides revenue certainty

shifts.trend: Total debt $21.6B at YE25 (avg ~10% rate) → $4.0B convert + $2.8B senior notes + $1.0B equity raised April 2026 — debt load still climbing
shifts.note: 10%+ average cost of debt on $21B+ is ~$2B/year interest burden on $5.1B FY25 revenue; cash flow negative

CRWV.verdict: Fastest-growing neocloud with RPO nearly at $100B. Diversification away from Microsoft is the key positive development. Leverage is extreme and the real risk — at 10% average borrowing cost on $21B+, the business must keep scaling rapidly to service debt.

CRM.headline: RPO re-accelerated from an apparent decline ($59.5B) to record $72.4B at year-end; cRPO outpacing total RPO growth (+16% vs +14%) signals near-term demand acceleration.

shifts.trend: RPO $59.5B (down from $63.4B peak in Jan 2025) at Q3 → $72.4B at FY26 end (+14% YoY); cRPO $35.1B (+16%) growing faster than total
shifts.note: The apparent Q3 trough was seasonal/timing; full-year shows strong demand recovery

shifts.trend: Revenue +9% Q3 → +10% FY26; operating margin ~19% Q3 → 20% FY26 — consistent execution
shifts.note: Agentforce 360 Platform +19% in Q3 is the only double-digit segment; accelerating AI/Data usage

shifts.trend: Agentforce 360 Platform (AI/Data Cloud) +19% Q3 → full platform rebranded; Informatica $9.6B closed Nov 2025, $0.4B FY26 contribution; Processor IP divestiture narrative
shifts.note: Informatica acquisition extending Data 360 agentic data layer — strategic positioning for AI agents

shifts.trend: Repurchased $12.7B (50M shares) + $1.6B dividends in FY26; OCF $15.0B (+15%) — strong cash generation deployed to buybacks
shifts.note: Capital allocation clarity: buy shares, invest in AI M&A, expand margins

CRM.verdict: Story intact. RPO recovery to record $72.4B and cRPO growing faster confirms the Agentforce AI transition is pulling near-term demand. Informatica integration adds data management at the agentic AI layer — strategically right but expensive.

NOW.headline: Consistent 20%+ subscription revenue growth maintained across both periods; RPO growing 25-27%; Now Assist AI agents embedded across all major product lines without separate disclosure.

shifts.trend: Subscription revenue +21% FY25 → +22% Q1 2026; total revenue consistent; RPO $28.2B (+27%) → $27.7B (+25%) — minor RPO moderation is seasonal
shifts.note: Pattern of steady acceleration; 20%+ growth at $13B scale is exceptional consistency

shifts.trend: cRPO ~$13.0B FY25 (+25%) → 46% of $27.7B Q1 (+23% YoY); self-hosted (federal) revenue growing as US government pushes on-prem deployments
shifts.note: Federal self-hosted demand ($492M FY25, growing) is a structural revenue segment usually underappreciated

shifts.trend: Now Assist AI agents embedded in ITSM/ITOM/CSM/HRSD/FSM/SecOps across both periods; no separate AI revenue disclosed — AI is the product not a surcharge
shifts.note: No AI monetization breakout limits visibility on true consumption uptake; watch for disclosure change

shifts.trend: Operating margin stable; subscription gross margin slightly pressured by self-hosted mix (ASC 985-20); overall structure unchanged across periods

NOW.verdict: Durable compounder at 20%+ growth. AI agents are embedded but not separately monetized — this is both the safety (no dependency on adoption) and the risk (no upside surprise visible). Federal self-hosted demand is an underappreciated growth driver.

SNOW.headline: Revenue growth held at 29-31% across both periods; NRR plateaued at 125%; customer base grew 21% to 13,328 with $1M+ customers up 27%; RPO grew $1.9B from Q3 to year-end.

shifts.trend: Revenue +29% Q3 → +31% FY26; $1M+ customers 576 (Q3 baseline) → 733 FY26 (+27%); total customers +21% to 13,328
shifts.note: Growth rate consistent; enterprise expansion the primary driver not new logo volume

shifts.trend: RPO $7.9B Q3 → $9.8B FY26 (+24% sequential from Q3 to Q4); 46% to recognize in next 12 months both periods
shifts.note: Sequential RPO build of $1.9B in Q4 alone signals strong new bookings into fiscal year-end

shifts.trend: Forbes Global 2000 customers stable at ~790 contributing ~43% of revenue; NRR 125% → 125% — plateaued at lower level than historical peak
shifts.note: NRR plateau at 125% vs historical 170%+ is the central debate; reflects consumption model maturity

shifts.trend: Net loss $1.3B FY26 with SBC declining to 34% of revenue (from 41%); OCF $1.2B positive — GAAP loss masks free cash flow generation
shifts.note: Path to profitability improving as SBC/revenue ratio declines; still several years from GAAP profitability

SNOW.verdict: Stable execution but NRR plateau at 125% is the core risk — it suggests consumption growth is maturing. The bull case rests on AI/data workloads driving a second wave of consumption acceleration; watch for NRR re-acceleration as the key signal.

PLTR.headline: Revenue growth accelerated from +53% FY25 to +85% Q1 2026; operating margin expanded from ~31% to 46%; commercial growth outpacing government at +95% vs +76%.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +53% FY25 → +85% Q1 2026; commercial +60% FY25 → +95% Q1; government +53% FY25 → +76% Q1
shifts.note: Most dramatic acceleration in the software/neoclouds layer; AIP bootcamp commercial traction converting

shifts.trend: Operating income $1.41B (31% margin) FY25 → $754M (46% margin) Q1 2026 (+328% YoY Q1); gross margin 80-81% FY25 → 87% Q1
shifts.note: Operating leverage is exceptional — revenue growing faster than cost base; ontology platform advantage showing

shifts.trend: US commercial strongest (+95%); US revenue 79% of total (vs 71% Q1 25); government contribution margin 73% (vs 62%) — both segments improving unit economics
shifts.note: US geography concentration consistent with deepening US commercial/gov penetration via AIP

shifts.trend: No customer >10% in Q1 2026 (and Q1 2025) — improving diversification vs government-heavy historical base
shifts.note: Customer diversification improving; large-customer dependency risk declining as commercial scales

PLTR.verdict: Explosive acceleration — +85% growth with 46% operating margin in Q1 is a remarkable print. AIP commercial traction is real. The key risk is sustainability: can this pace continue or is there a pull-forward effect from AIP bootcamp conversions?

CDNS.headline: Revenue growth accelerated from +14% to +19%; China revenue +36% in Q1 despite BIS overhang; backlog grew modestly to $8.0B; operating margin held at 28-29%.

shifts.trend: Revenue $5.30B (+14%) FY25 → $1.47B (+19%) Q1 FY26; Core EDA leading growth; geographic growth broad-based in Q1 (Americas +87%, Japan +36%)
shifts.note: Acceleration despite China BIS noise; AI chip design demand underlying the growth

shifts.trend: China $680M (+19%) FY25 → $189M (+36%) Q1 FY26; BIS 'is-informed' letter imposed May 23, rescinded Jul 2, 2025 — no lasting impact on revenue
shifts.note: China growing faster in Q1 than FY25 pace despite Sept 2025 BIS 50%-owned-affiliate rule; mgmt expects 'limited' impact

shifts.trend: Backlog $7.8B FY25 → $8.0B Q1 (+2.6%); 53% → 55% recognizable in next 12mo; non-cancelable FSA commitments $0.7B in Q1
shifts.note: Backlog slowly building; short-cycle recognition rate increasing

shifts.trend: Operating margin 28% FY25 (pressured by $128.5M BIS/DOJ settlement) → 29% Q1 FY26; FY25 settlement was one-time
shifts.note: Core operating margin actually improved once settlement is excluded; Hexagon D&E to add amortization

CDNS.verdict: Steady acceleration with China resilience. Hexagon D&E will bring amortization headwinds but also simulation/physics revenue diversification. EDA demand for AI chip design is structural; BIS policy risk remains the key tail risk.

SNPS.headline: Ansys closed July 2025 driving +66% Q1 revenue; Design IP segment weak both periods; Processor IP divested to GlobalFoundries; $3.5B term loan repaid in Q1.

shifts.trend: Revenue +15% FY25 (ex-Ansys organic partially offset by China -22%) → +66% Q1 FY26 ($885.6M Ansys inorganic); Design Automation +96% Q1
shifts.note: Organic growth masked by Ansys contribution; underlying EDA demand healthy ex-Design IP

shifts.trend: Design IP segment: weak FY25 (BIS restrictions, roadmap missteps) → -6% Q1 FY26; muted growth expected all of FY26; Processor IP divested to GF
shifts.note: Design IP is the structural weak link; management is pruning it (GF divestiture) to focus on higher-growth EDA

shifts.trend: Backlog $11.4B FY25 → $11.3B Q1 (flat — stable at high level); 45% → 47% recognizable in next 12mo
shifts.note: Backlog stability post-Ansys close is reassuring; integration not disrupting bookings

shifts.trend: $13.5B debt at FY25-end; $3.5B term loan repaid Q1 FY26; buyback still suspended pending debt paydown; $300-350M restructuring plan underway
shifts.note: Deleveraging trajectory positive; buyback resumption expected as debt normalizes to target

SNPS.verdict: Ansys integration is the story; organic EDA demand is healthy but Design IP is a drag. Debt paydown is ahead of schedule; once leverage normalizes and restructuring completes, buyback resumption should be the next catalyst.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition closed Jan 7, 2026 — Constellation became the world's largest private power producer at 55 GW; Crane (TMI) DOE loan guarantee secured; AI baseload demand cited as multi-decade tailwind.

shifts.trend: Pre-close: operating revenues ~$6B 9M run-rate; FY25 post-close structure: $25.5B revenue (includes Calpine Q4); adjusted EPS $9.39 FY25 vs $8.67 FY24
shifts.note: Calpine dramatically changes the revenue scale; FY26 will be the first full year of the combined entity

shifts.trend: Nuclear fleet: Crane (TMI restart) $1B DOE loan guarantee secured Nov 2025; Clinton 20-yr Meta PPA + 30 MW uprate; NY PSC 20-yr ZEC extension Jan 2026; 80-yr license extensions planned
shifts.note: Nuclear contract signed with Microsoft (Crane), Meta (Clinton), and NY ZEC — tech demand driving long-duration PPAs

shifts.trend: Calpine adds 27 GW natural gas + 730 MW geothermal + 800 MW battery; combined 55 GW serving 2.5M retail accounts including 75% of Fortune 100
shifts.note: AI data center baseload demand explicitly named by management as multi-decade tailwind for nuclear fleet

shifts.trend: Calpine acquired for ~$22B (50M shares + $4.5B cash); debt load increased substantially; rating and leverage trajectory to watch post-close
shifts.note: M&A at this scale significantly increases financial leverage; integration execution is the primary near-term risk

CEG.verdict: Structural transformation completed. Constellation is now the dominant US clean baseload power platform. AI-driven data center demand for 24/7 carbon-free power is the thesis; the risk is integration execution and leverage at the new scale.

OKLO.headline: Meta prepayment agreement signed Jan 2026 for 1.2 GW Pike County campus; order book remains ~18,000+ MWe; FY26 cash burn $80-100M opex + $350-450M capex as construction begins.

shifts.trend: Order book ~18,100 MWe Q3 FY25; Meta 1.2 GW Pike County prepayment agreement Jan 2026 — first hyperscaler prepayment for advanced fission; Switch 12 GW master agreement still in place
shifts.note: Hyperscaler pre-payment (not just LOI) is a credibility step-change for the commercial timeline

shifts.trend: FY25 opex $139.3M (+164% YoY); FY26 guidance $80-100M opex + $350-450M capex (new capex guidance vs no prior capex line); Pike County land acquired Dec 2025
shifts.note: First year with meaningful capex guidance signals transition from pre-commercial to construction phase

shifts.trend: Still pre-revenue (no product revenue in either period); 9M FY25 cash burn $48.7M operations + $605.9M investing (mainly marketable securities); $1.18B cash Q3 2025
shifts.note: Investing in securities not capex in FY25; FY26 capex guidance marks the shift to physical construction

shifts.trend: NRC Phase I pre-application readiness passed with no significant gaps Sept 2025; Aurora DOE Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved Feb 2026; accelerated timeline cited
shifts.note: Regulatory progress is faster than historical nuclear timeframes — the positive surprise in the filings

OKLO.verdict: Commercial validation progressing — Meta prepayment and NRC momentum are meaningful milestones. The thesis is about whether Aurora can deploy by 2028 at commercial scale; regulatory progress supports it; execution remains speculative at this stage.

LEU.headline: Backlog grew to $3.9B; DOE $900M HALEU task order won Jan 2026; SWU spot price $200 vs $34 historic low (+488%); Fluor EPC contract signed and Oklo JV exploration launched.

shifts.trend: SWU spot price $200 at Q1 end vs $34 historic low — enrichment market is at price levels not seen in decades; backlog $3.8B → $3.9B extending to 2040
shifts.note: Non-Russian enrichment market undersupplied by any reasonable future-demand scenario

shifts.trend: Technical Solutions $32.1M Q1 (+47% YoY) on HALEU Operation Contract; LEU segment $44.6M (-13%, SWU volume -47% but avg price +52%)
shifts.note: Revenue growing slowly relative to backlog size — contingent contracts ($2.4B) not yet monetized pending new capacity

shifts.trend: Advanced technology costs jumped to $18.9M Q1 (vs $3.0M, +530%) from Piketon/Oak Ridge expansion; Fluor EPC contract signed Feb 2026; first new centrifuges expected Ohio 2029
shifts.note: $800M+ multi-year Oak Ridge centrifuge manufacturing investment beginning to hit P&L

shifts.trend: $900M DOE HALEU task order won Jan 2026; Oklo JV exploration for HALEU deconversion at Piketon announced March 2026 — Oklo 1.2 GW campus co-located
shifts.note: Oklo strategic alignment (co-located fuel recycling + power campus) could unlock long-duration contract structure

LEU.verdict: Unique positioning: only NRC-licensed commercial HALEU enricher with a backlog extending to 2040. The risk is funding dependence on Congressional appropriations (FY27 budget proposal lacks HALEU cascade funding). SWU pricing at $200 is strongly supportive but capacity build-out is multi-year.
